evals.prompt: Build a React TypeScript component for a user profile card that shows avatar, name, email, and a bio. It should have an Edit button that opens an inline edit form. Use React Hook Form for the form. Show loading and error states.
evals.expected_output: TypeScript React component(s) with typed props, React Hook Form integration, loading/error/edit states, and accessible markup.
expectations: Output is TypeScript (.tsx) not plain JavaScript
Props interface is defined with typed fields
React Hook Form is used for the edit form (useForm hook visible)
Component handles at least loading and error states
Edit mode toggles between view and edit inline
Accessible attributes are present (aria-label, button type, label associations)
File path is shown as a comment at the top

evals.prompt: Create a Next.js App Router page for a product listing page that fetches products from /api/products server-side. Show a skeleton loading state. Products have name, price, and category. Support filtering by category via URL search params.
evals.expected_output: Next.js App Router page using Server Components for data fetching, with category filter via searchParams and skeleton loading via Suspense.
expectations: Page component is a Server Component (no 'use client' at the top level)
Data is fetched using async/await directly in the component or a lib function
searchParams are used for category filtering
Suspense boundary is used with a fallback skeleton
Product list renders name, price, and category
The filtering UI (category selector) is a separate 'use client' component or uses a form
File path follows Next.js App Router convention (app/...)
